blocks.html_en: What they do is put lipstick on a pig! There is no need to "reduce" the risk of SQL injections when you can use a safe API that eliminates the entire error class. This is a solved problem for the rest of the world!

sentences.en: What they do is put lipstick on a pig!
sentences.zh: 他们所做的就是给猪涂口红！

sentences.en: There is no need to "reduce" the risk of SQL injections when you can use a safe API that eliminates the entire error class.
sentences.zh: 当您可以使用消除整个错误类的安全 API 时，无需“降低”SQL 注入的风险。

sentences.en: This is a solved problem for the rest of the world!
sentences.zh: 对于世界其他地区来说，这是一个已解决的问题！

blocks.html_en:
PHP offers that safe API, though, and always has (prepared statements). The same one as all other languages.

PHP might seem worse than other languages due to a combination of factors:

- It's the most used one by far, even though few of us like to admit it.

- Old tutorials still come up during web searches, so "SELECT * FROM `table` WHERE id = $id" will still be written today.

sentences.en: PHP offers that safe API, though, and always has (prepared statements).

blocks.html_en:
I never really understood these complaints about the standard library, that's not what makes a language really. Yes, it's ugly, yes, it carries 30 years of baggage, but it's PHP the language that allows you to interact with a much more convenient abstraction layer provided by Laravel.

PHP can run the same code fully dynamically typed or with very strict type annotations, depending on your requirements. It has runtime reflection APIs that are so cheap that you don't really have to think about using them. You can do OOP or FP with PHP, or even procedural HTML-interleaved-with-PHP if that's your thing. It has late static binding, so you can defer to child classes from their parent class. There are generators and fibres as first-class language constructs now. Property hooks are an extremely clear pattern, way better than in many other languages.

Generally, there have been tons of new syntax extensions over the years, and they all slot in gracefully. With PHP 8.6, we're going to get partial application for functions, which will make PHP 8.5's match expressions one of the most ergonomic implementations I have seen yet!

sentences.en: I never really understood these complaints about the standard library, that's not what makes a language really.
sentences.zh: 我从来没有真正理解这些对标准库的抱怨，这并不是真正的语言。

sentences.en: Yes, it's ugly, yes, it carries 30 years of baggage, but it's PHP the language that allows you to interact with a much more convenient abstraction layer provided by Laravel.PHP can run the same code fully dynamically typed or with very strict type annotations, depending on your requirements.
sentences.zh: 是的，它很丑陋，是的，它承载了 30 年的包袱，但 PHP 是一种允许您与 Laravel 提供的更方便的抽象层进行交互的语言。PHP 可以完全动态类型化或使用非常严格的类型注释运行相同的代码，具体取决于您的要求。

sentences.en: It has runtime reflection APIs that are so cheap that you don't really have to think about using them.
sentences.zh: 它具有非常便宜的运行时反射 API，您实际上不必考虑使用它们。

sentences.en: You can do OOP or FP with PHP, or even procedural HTML-interleaved-with-PHP if that's your thing.
sentences.zh: 您可以使用 PHP 进行 OOP 或 FP，甚至可以使用 PHP 进行过程式 HTML 交错（如果您喜欢的话）。

sentences.en: It has late static binding, so you can defer to child classes from their parent class.
sentences.zh: 它具有后期静态绑定，因此您可以遵循父类的子类。

sentences.en: There are generators and fibres as first-class language constructs now.
sentences.zh: 现在有生成器和纤程作为一流的语言构造。

sentences.en: Property hooks are an extremely clear pattern, way better than in many other languages.Generally, there have been tons of new syntax extensions over the years, and they all slot in gracefully.
sentences.zh: 属性挂钩是一种极其清晰的模式，比许多其他语言都要好。通常，这些年来出现了大量新的语法扩展，并且它们都优雅地插入。

sentences.en: With PHP 8.6, we're going to get partial application for functions, which will make PHP 8.5's match expressions one of the most ergonomic implementations I have seen yet!
sentences.zh: 在 PHP 8.6 中，我们将获得函数的部分应用，这将使 PHP 8.5 的匹配表达式成为我见过的最符合人体工程学的实现之一！

blocks.html_en:
> PHP doesn't suffer from that because there are barely any libraries for it.

You don't need many libraries in a typical project, because PHP is batteries-included and if you use a framework it does all the rest for you, that is true. But there are still hundreds of thousands of packages with billions of installs:

https://packagist.org/statistics

blocks.html_en:
Let me convince you! :-)

There are match expressions and arrow functions:

  $slug = $title
      |> trim(...)
      |> (fn($str) => str_replace(' ', '-', $str))
      |> (fn($str) => str_replace('.', '', $str))
      |> strtolower(...);

There is meta-programming with annotations:

  final class PostsController
  {
      #[AccessControl(fn(Request $request, Post $post)
          => $request->user === $post->getAuthor()
      )]
      public function update(Request $request, Post $post): Response {
          // ...
      }
  }

Native (and optionally value-backed) enums:

  enum Status
  {
      case Draft;
      case Published;
      case Archived;
  }

Proper class property hooks, fully replacing getters and setters:

  class Data
  {
      public string $fullName {
          get => "{$this->firstName} {$this->lastName}";
          set(string $value) {
              [$first, $last] = explode(' ', $value);
              $this->firstName = $first;
              $this->lastName = $last;
          }
      }
  }

And tons of other features—among them asymmetric property visibility, fibres/green threads, DNF types, lazy object instantiation, an ever-improving yet fully opt-in static type system with runtime validation, a JIT compiler, an amazing package ecosystem and -manager, annotation-backed deprecation, and more.

blocks.html_en:
Let me convince you! :-)

There are match expressions and arrow functions:

  $slug = $title
      |> trim(...)
      |> (fn($str) => str_replace(' ', '-', $str))
      |> (fn($str) => str_replace('.', '', $str))
      |> strtolower(...);

There is meta-programming with annotations:

  final class PostsController
  {
      #[AccessControl(fn(Request $request, Post $post)
          => $request->user === $post->getAuthor()
      )]
      public function update(Request $request, Post $post): Response {
          // ...
      }
  }

Native (and optionally value-backed) enums:

  enum Status
  {
      case Draft;
      case Published;
      case Archived;
  }

              $this->lastName = $last;
          }
      }
  }

And tons of other features—among them asymmetric property visibility, fibres/green threads, DNF types, lazy object instantiation, an ever-improving yet fully opt-in static type system with runtime validation, a JIT compiler, an amazing package ecosystem and -manager, annotation-backed deprecation, and more.

blocks.html_en:
> There is meta-programming with annotations:

There is, but your example is not valid. You can't use short function syntax in constant expressions, it has to be an explicit `static function`.

The reason for this, IIRC (I can't find the GitHub issue or PR atm) is that these functions aren't allowed to close over any variables, and with fn syntax doing so is implicit. Rather than erroring they've disallowed the use of arrow functions entirely.

sentences.en: > There is meta-programming with annotations:There is, but your example is not valid.
sentences.zh: > 有带注释的元编程：有，但您的示例无效。

sentences.en: You can't use short function syntax in constant expressions, it has to be an explicit `static function`.The reason for this, IIRC (I can't find the GitHub issue or PR atm) is that these functions aren't allowed to close over any variables, and with fn syntax doing so is implicit.
sentences.zh: 你不能在常量表达式中使用短函数语法，它必须是显式的“静态函数”。IIRC（我找不到 GitHub 问题或 PR atm）的原因是这些函数不允许关闭任何变量，并且使用 fn 语法这样做是隐式的。

sentences.en: Rather than erroring they've disallowed the use of arrow functions entirely.
sentences.zh: 他们没有犯错误，而是完全禁止使用箭头函数。

blocks.html_en:
These "very new things" have, in part, been available for more than 6 years now. Not to speak of all the other PHP features WordPress chose to forgo, like PDO with prepared statements (which completely eliminates SQL injections), which came out 21 freakin' years ago.

There are many very successful and professional PHP projects with millions of users that seem to be able to migrate to newer PHP versions just fine.

Whatever the WordPress developers are claiming are the reasons for their irresponsible codebase and their inability to improve it is nobody's fault but their own.

sentences.en: These "very new things" have, in part, been available for more than 6 years now.
sentences.zh: 这些“非常新的东西”在某种程度上已经面世六年多了。

sentences.en: Not to speak of all the other PHP features WordPress chose to forgo, like PDO with prepared statements (which completely eliminates SQL injections), which came out 21 freakin' years ago.There are many very successful and professional PHP projects with millions of users that seem to be able to migrate to newer PHP versions just fine.Whatever the WordPress developers are claiming are the reasons for their irresponsible codebase and their inability to improve it is nobody's fault but their own.
sentences.zh: 更不用说 WordPress 选择放弃的所有其他 PHP 功能了，比如 21 年前推出的带预准备语句的 PDO（完全消除了 SQL 注入）。有许多非常成功且专业的 PHP 项目，拥有数百万用户，似乎能够很好地迁移到较新的 PHP 版本。无论 WordPress 开发人员声称什么，他们不负责任的代码库和无法改进代码库的原因都不是别人的错，而是他们自己的错。

blocks.html_en:
One usually uses "query builder" pattern for that.

Also, regarding placeholders, historically many DB and frameworks do not support passing lists for a value in a placeholder (like "WHERE id IN(?)") so users of such software fall back to string concatenation.

sentences.en: One usually uses "query builder" pattern for that.Also, regarding placeholders, historically many DB and frameworks do not support passing lists for a value in a placeholder (like "WHERE id IN(?)") so users of such software fall back to string concatenation.
sentences.zh: 人们通常使用“查询生成器”模式来实现这一点。此外，关于占位符，历史上许多数据库和框架不支持在占位符中传递值的列表（例如“WHERE id IN（？）”），因此此类软件的用户会退回到字符串连接。

blocks.html_en: Not user code, no. Someone eventually has to, but virtually every ORM under the sun allows you to construct dynamic queries without having to concatenate strings yourself or resort to string interpolation.

blocks.html_en:
Quick search shows this in Wordpress:

> WordPress database access abstraction class.

  class wpdb {}

So this is some sort of ORM provided.

  $results = $wpdb->get_results( "SELECT * FROM {$wpdb->prefix}options WHERE option_id = 1", OBJECT );

> Some of the methods in this class take an SQL statement as input. All untrusted values in an SQL statement must be escaped to prevent SQL injection attacks. Some methods will escape SQL for you; others will not. Check the documentation to be sure before you use any method in this class. For more on SQL escaping in WordPress, see the section entitled Protect Queries Against SQL Injection Attacks below.

It does not however prevent $wpdb users from NOT binding query parameters, which leads to this vulnerability.

sentences.en: Quick search shows this in Wordpress:> WordPress database access abstraction class.
sentences.zh: 快速搜索在 WordPress:> WordPress 数据库访问抽象类中显示了这一点。

sentences.en: class wpdb {} So this is some sort of ORM provided. $results = $wpdb->get_results( "SELECT * FROM {$wpdb->prefix}options WHERE option_id = 1", OBJECT ); > Some of the methods in this class take an SQL statement as input.
sentences.zh: class wpdb {} 所以这是提供的某种 ORM。 $results = $wpdb->get_results( "SELECT * FROM {$wpdb->prefix}options WHERE option_id = 1", OBJECT ); > 此类中的一些方法采用 SQL 语句作为输入。

sentences.en: All untrusted values in an SQL statement must be escaped to prevent SQL injection attacks.
sentences.zh: SQL 语句中所有不受信任的值都必须进行转义，以防止 SQL 注入攻击。

sentences.en: Some methods will escape SQL for you; others will not.
sentences.zh: 有些方法会为你转义 SQL；其他人则不会。

sentences.en: Check the documentation to be sure before you use any method in this class.
sentences.zh: 在使用此类中的任何方法之前，请检查文档以确保正确。

sentences.en: For more on SQL escaping in WordPress, see the section entitled Protect Queries Against SQL Injection Attacks below.It does not however prevent $wpdb users from NOT binding query parameters, which leads to this vulnerability.
sentences.zh: 有关 WordPress 中 SQL 转义的更多信息，请参阅下面标题为“保护查询免受 SQL 注入攻击”的部分。但是，它并不能阻止 $wpdb 用户不绑定查询参数，从而导致此漏洞。

sentences.en: So the developers have to fall back to string concatenation.

blocks.html_en: I am so done with FOMO writing. Sure man, you found one with $25. With $25 plus your entire industry domain specific knowledge of where to look, of how to probe, of what else you may have accumulated and collected over the years of working within this industry. Let's stop with the gambling narrative and the illusion that we are all missing out.

sentences.en: I am so done with FOMO writing.
sentences.zh: 我已经厌倦了 FOMO 写作。

sentences.en: Sure man, you found one with $25.
sentences.zh: 当然，伙计，你花了 25 美元找到了一个。

sentences.en: With $25 plus your entire industry domain specific knowledge of where to look, of how to probe, of what else you may have accumulated and collected over the years of working within this industry.
sentences.zh: 只需 25 美元，再加上您在整个行业领域的特定知识，包括在哪里寻找、如何探索以及您在该行业工作多年中可能积累和收集的其他知识。

sentences.en: Let's stop with the gambling narrative and the illusion that we are all missing out.
sentences.zh: 让我们停止赌博的叙述和我们都错过了的幻想。

blocks.html_en: I am so done with FOMO writing. Sure man, you found one with $25. blocks.html_en:
I agree. This is so toxic! It feels like the Instagram of articles. "Look how my life is great" - yeah sure, you're posting only happy moments.

Not only $25 is not accounting the years of experience, but also all the failed attempts.

sentences.zh: 我同意。

sentences.en: This is so toxic!
sentences.zh: 这太有毒了！

sentences.en: It feels like the Instagram of articles.

blocks.html_en:
I still don't understand why, for a blog, a static page isn't enough - especially since most of the WordPress issues are "solved" by adding caching.

I do understand it from an user perspective (it's easier to tell the average user to drag and drop rather than committing to a GitHub repo and letting hugo build the website), but from a security standpoint WordPress is really just waiting for a vulnerability (either in the core or on the thousands of plugins) in order to unlock its RCE-as-a-service functionality.

sentences.en: I still don't understand why, for a blog, a static page isn't enough - especially since most of the WordPress issues are "solved" by adding caching.I do understand it from an user perspective (it's easier to tell the average user to drag and drop rather than committing to a GitHub repo and letting hugo build the website), but from a security standpoint WordPress is really just waiting for a vulnerability (either in the core or on the thousands of plugins) in order to unlock its RCE-as-a-service functionality.
sentences.zh: 我仍然不明白为什么对于博客来说，静态页面是不够的 - 特别是因为大多数 WordPress 问题都是通过添加缓存来“解决”的。我确实从用户的角度理解它（告诉普通用户拖放比提交到 GitHub 存储库并让 Hugo 构建网站更容易），但从安全角度来看，WordPress 实际上只是在等待漏洞（无论是在核心中还是在数千个插件中）来解锁其RCE 即服务功能。

blocks.html_en: In many deployments, Wordpress started out as just a blog that is easy to edit right from the web browser, but then grew into way more. Most commonly, people end up retrofitting all kinds of e-commerce features onto it, and that's how you really get into the whole plugin mess. At that point, for better or worse, the Wordpress instance is serving important business needs that are not addressed by a static page.

sentences.en: In many deployments, Wordpress started out as just a blog that is easy to edit right from the web browser, but then grew into way more.
sentences.zh: 在许多部署中，Wordpress 最初只是一个可以直接从 Web 浏览器轻松编辑的博客，但后来发展得越来越广泛。

sentences.en: Most commonly, people end up retrofitting all kinds of e-commerce features onto it, and that's how you really get into the whole plugin mess.
sentences.zh: 最常见的是，人们最终会在其上改装各种电子商务功能，这就是您真正陷入整个插件混乱的方式。

sentences.en: At that point, for better or worse, the Wordpress instance is serving important business needs that are not addressed by a static page.
sentences.zh: 此时，无论好坏，Wordpress 实例正在满足静态页面无法满足的重要业务需求。

blocks.html_en: People like having a WYSIWYG editor, being able to update their posts in the browser, having comments and being able to use plugins. They're also not particularly skilled with the terminal/commands, and a lot of hosts provide a one-click install setup for scripts like WordPress.

sentences.en: People like having a WYSIWYG editor, being able to update their posts in the browser, having comments and being able to use plugins.
sentences.zh: 人们喜欢拥有一个所见即所得的编辑器，能够在浏览器中更新他们的帖子，发表评论并能够使用插件。

sentences.en: They're also not particularly skilled with the terminal/commands, and a lot of hosts provide a one-click install setup for scripts like WordPress.
sentences.zh: 他们对终端/命令也不是特别熟练，并且许多主机为 WordPress 等脚本提供一键安装设置。

blocks.html_en:
Like you said. It is a product for people who are not technical and don't code. A product that you would have to use git for, wouldn't be used by the 90 percent in the first place. And there are such products. There is a reason WordPress is the most popular platform. Most people are not technical.

And people like to be able to extend from Blogs to various other non static features which WordPress allows for.

blocks.html_en: Its so funny… our wordpress started off as simple blogging thing but now is not only using 99% cpu but also no one knows how to edit/deal with all the plugins etc. Wordpress is the worst choice. Always

blocks.html_en:
I've got it at 38.32%. I'm looking at the 'front-door' of the web, active sites, no subdomains, from Crux user experience data.

Will be publishing a report on www.theweb.report soon - if you're interested.

That's a seed of about 13 million domains - pretty sure WordPress would be dominating the even longer tail.

( Also worth noting it's sooo easy to detect a site is WordPress, it screams it across every signal we gather, where-as some sites are just well made and have limited information leaks ).

sentences.en: I've got it at 38.32%.
sentences.zh: 我已经拿到38.32%了。

sentences.en: I'm looking at the 'front-door' of the web, active sites, no subdomains, from Crux user experience data.Will be publishing a report on www.theweb.report soon - if you're interested.That's a seed of about 13 million domains - pretty sure WordPress would be dominating the even longer tail.( Also worth noting it's sooo easy to detect a site is WordPress, it screams it across every signal we gather, where-as some sites are just well made and have limited information leaks ).
sentences.zh: 我正在从 Crux 用户体验数据中查看网络的“前门”，活跃站点，没有子域。很快就会在 www.theweb.report 上发布一份报告 - 如果您感兴趣的话。这是一个大约 1300 万个域的种子 - 很肯定 WordPress 将占据更长的尾巴。（还值得注意的是，WordPress 很容易检测到站点，它会在我们收集到的每个信号中尖叫，因为有些站点制作精良并且具有有限的信息泄露）。

blocks.html_en:
Interesting write-up and I do think LLM assisted/powered exploit disclosure is a real concern (I've been able to get models to create container breakouts from Linux LPEs relatively quickly).

One thing I'm surprised about is that GPT-5.6 didn't block that prompt due to guardrails. My experience is that GPT-5.5 and up does not like offensive security work (similar to Opus 4.7+/Fable).

I didn't notice it but I'd assume that the authors have some level of cyber approvals from OpenAI to relax the guardrails a bit.

sentences.en: Interesting write-up and I do think LLM assisted/powered exploit disclosure is a real concern (I've been able to get models to create container breakouts from Linux LPEs relatively quickly).One thing I'm surprised about is that GPT-5.6 didn't block that prompt due to guardrails.
sentences.zh: 有趣的文章，我确实认为 LLM 辅助/支持的漏洞披露是一个真正值得关注的问题（我已经能够相对快速地获得从 Linux LPE 创建容器突破的模型）。令我惊讶的一件事是，GPT-5.6 并没有因为护栏而阻止该提示。

sentences.en: My experience is that GPT-5.5 and up does not like offensive security work (similar to Opus 4.7+/Fable).I didn't notice it but I'd assume that the authors have some level of cyber approvals from OpenAI to relax the guardrails a bit.
sentences.zh: 我的经验是，GPT-5.5 及更高版本不喜欢进攻性安全工作（类似于 Opus 4.7+/Fable）。我没有注意到这一点，但我假设作者获得了 OpenAI 的某种程度的网络批准，可以稍微放松一些防护。

blocks.html_en: I feel like I've seen plenty of non-AI, pre-2020 SAST tools catch SQLis like the one mentioned here, and if nothing else, then a code review ought to catch it. Is WordPress not using code reviews and/or SAST?

sentences.en: I feel like I've seen plenty of non-AI, pre-2020 SAST tools catch SQLis like the one mentioned here, and if nothing else, then a code review ought to catch it.
sentences.zh: 我觉得我已经看到很多非 AI、2020 年之前的 SAST 工具可以捕获 SQL，就像这里提到的那样，如果没有其他问题，那么代码审查应该可以捕获它。

blocks.html_en:
One of my websites was hacked with this, luckily not one with any users at all.

They did this:

- Two admin accounts in the database.

- plugin dir: wp-content/plugins/wp-core with remote command-execution web shell wp-core-[12 random chars].php

- firewall.php backdoor in mu-plugins dir with admin on GET ?sergei

- cache-seo-helper.php backdoor

- fixer.php which renames the wordpress version number to one which is patched.

I have decided to give up on Wordpress.

sentences.en: One of my websites was hacked with this, luckily not one with any users at all.They did this:- Two admin accounts in the database.- plugin dir: wp-content/plugins/wp-core with remote command-execution web shell wp-core-[12 random chars].php- firewall.php backdoor in mu-plugins dir with admin on GET ?sergei- cache-seo-helper.php backdoor- fixer.php which renames the wordpress version number to one which is patched.I have decided to give up on Wordpress.
sentences.zh: 我的一个网站被黑客攻击了，幸运的是根本没有任何用户。他们这样做了：- 数据库中有两个管理员帐户。- 插件目录：wp-content/plugins/wp-core，带有远程命令执行 web shell wp-core-[12 个随机字符].php- mu-plugins 目录中的firewall.php 后门，管理员在 GET ?sergei-cache-seo-helper.php backdoor-fixer.php 上重命名将 WordPress 版本号更改为已修补的版本号。我已决定放弃 WordPress。

blocks.html_en: The author lost me at the last bit where they started using weird names for the posts. Why would you make one ID O and the other ID 0? Why single letters and not EMBED_01? Why seemingly random letters instead of ABCDEF? Does OCPDST stand for something?

sentences.zh: 作者在最后一点让我迷失了，他们开始为帖子使用奇怪的名称。

sentences.en: Why would you make one ID O and the other ID 0?
sentences.zh: 为什么要将一个 ID 设置为 O，另一个 ID 设置为 0？

sentences.en: Why single letters and not EMBED_01?
sentences.zh: 为什么是单个字母而不是 EMBED_01？

sentences.en: Why seemingly random letters instead of ABCDEF?
sentences.zh: 为什么看似随机的字母而不是ABCDEF？

sentences.en: Does OCPDST stand for something?
sentences.zh: OCPDST 代表什么吗？

blocks.html_en:
They are placeholders, their meanings are spelled out in the post:

    O: publish/oembed_cache, empty content, stale timestamp with parent C
    C: future/customize_changeset, changeset JSON with parent C
    P: draft/page, with parent D
    D: parse/request with itself as its parent
    S: publish/post, for providing embed data
    T: publish/post, containing the outer embed

sentences.en: They are placeholders, their meanings are spelled out in the post: O: publish/oembed_cache, empty content, stale timestamp with parent C C: future/customize_changeset, changeset JSON with parent C P: draft/page, with parent D D: parse/request with itself as its parent S: publish/post, for providing embed data T: publish/post, containing the outer embed
sentences.zh: 它们是占位符，它们的含义在帖子中阐明： O：publish/oembed_cache，空内容，父级 C 的过时时间戳 C：future/customize_changeset，父级 C 的变更集 JSON P：草稿/页面，父级 D D：解析/请求，以其自身作为其父级 S：发布/发布，用于提供嵌入数据 T：发布/发布，包含外部嵌入

blocks.html_en: I might be missing something, so perhaps someone can explain: Why are the steps in the middle of the exploit chain necessary? The writeup describes getting a SQL injection, then going from there to cache poisoning to exploiting various logic bugs, to eventually creating an admin account (and WordPress grants RCE to admin accounts by design). But if you have a SQL injection, why can't you use that to just create an admin account directly, by inserting it into the users table?

sentences.en: I might be missing something, so perhaps someone can explain: Why are the steps in the middle of the exploit chain necessary?
sentences.zh: 我可能会遗漏一些东西，所以也许有人可以解释：为什么漏洞利用链中间的步骤是必要的？

sentences.en: The writeup describes getting a SQL injection, then going from there to cache poisoning to exploiting various logic bugs, to eventually creating an admin account (and WordPress grants RCE to admin accounts by design).
sentences.zh: 这篇文章描述了如何进行 SQL 注入，然后从那里开始缓存中毒，再到利用各种逻辑错误，最后创建一个管理员帐户（WordPress 按设计向管理员帐户授予 RCE）。

sentences.en: But if you have a SQL injection, why can't you use that to just create an admin account directly, by inserting it into the users table?
sentences.zh: 但是，如果您有 SQL 注入，为什么不能使用它直接创建一个管理员帐户，将其插入到用户表中呢？

blocks.html_en:
Then why didn't we see the same writeup earlier? If you look through the writeup, you still need the skills to go through what the LLM gives you and actually create a valid proof of concept.

I've been using LLMs to find security vulnerabilities and there is no way I can just submit what I found and call it a day (many try).

sentences.en: Then why didn't we see the same writeup earlier?
sentences.zh: 那为什么我们之前没有看到同样的文章呢？

sentences.en: If you look through the writeup, you still need the skills to go through what the LLM gives you and actually create a valid proof of concept.I've been using LLMs to find security vulnerabilities and there is no way I can just submit what I found and call it a day (many try).
sentences.zh: 如果你仔细阅读这篇文章，你仍然需要技能来了解法学硕士为你提供的内容，并真正创建一个有效的概念证明。我一直在使用法学硕士来查找安全漏洞，但我不可能只提交我发现的内容并收工（很多尝试）。

blocks.html_en:
You read articles regularly about how X authority is provided cloud LLM history and uses it as evidence to prosecute a defendant.

Yet you think professional criminals are too stupid to launder their activities through an unscrupulous yet legal intermediary?

blocks.html_en:
> Is GPT5.6 Sol Superhuman?

This is not a simple y/n question. Computers have been superhuman at playing chess for decades now. Reading this article, I guess they are superhuman at understanding code now as well.

sentences.en: > Is GPT5.6 Sol Superhuman?This is not a simple y/n question.

sentences.en: Computers have been superhuman at playing chess for decades now.
sentences.zh: 几十年来，计算机下国际象棋的能力一直超人。

sentences.en: Reading this article, I guess they are superhuman at understanding code now as well.
sentences.zh: 读了这篇文章，我猜他们现在在理解代码方面也超人了。

blocks.html_en:
That was an incredible writeup! Chapeau to the author - thanks for taking the time to do a writeup.

When Anthropic claimed Mythos chained 4 or 5 bugs to achieve sandbox escape and found bugs in core software, it sounded like bs. But here we are 2 months later seeing what they meant.

Cybersecurity was always a hard sell; security flaws were invisible - by contrast a fence with a hole is visible to everyone - anyone can ignore the locked gate and walk through the fence hole. With cybersecurity a hole in the fence may go unnoticed for years, maybe forever.

LLMs level the field. We will all benefit from more secure systems, a few people will get a lot of egg on their faces, and it will end the malpractice of underinvesting in software security to get a product out the door.

sentences.en: That was an incredible writeup!
sentences.zh: 那是一篇令人难以置信的文章！

sentences.en: Chapeau to the author - thanks for taking the time to do a writeup.When Anthropic claimed Mythos chained 4 or 5 bugs to achieve sandbox escape and found bugs in core software, it sounded like bs.
sentences.zh: 作者致谢 - 感谢您花时间写这篇文章。当 Anthropic 声称 Mythos 链接了 4 或 5 个 bug 来实现沙箱逃逸并在核心软件中发现了 bug 时，这听起来像是废话。

sentences.en: But here we are 2 months later seeing what they meant.Cybersecurity was always a hard sell; security flaws were invisible - by contrast a fence with a hole is visible to everyone - anyone can ignore the locked gate and walk through the fence hole.
sentences.zh: 但两个月后，我们才明白了他们的意思。网络安全一直是一个很难推销的东西；安全缺陷是看不见的——相比之下，每个人都可以看到带洞的栅栏——任何人都可以忽略上锁的门并穿过栅栏洞。

sentences.en: With cybersecurity a hole in the fence may go unnoticed for years, maybe forever.LLMs level the field.
sentences.zh: 对于网络安全来说，栅栏上的一个漏洞可能会被忽视很多年，甚至永远不会被注意到。法学硕士可以平衡这个领域。

sentences.en: We will all benefit from more secure systems, a few people will get a lot of egg on their faces, and it will end the malpractice of underinvesting in software security to get a product out the door.
sentences.zh: 我们都将从更安全的系统中受益，少数人会脸上蒙上一层鸡蛋，这将结束为推出产品而在软件安全方面投资不足的弊端。

blocks.html_en: This one is both awesome and scary. We are living in a black mirror episode, where one well-crafted LLM prompt can get you $500k or the ability to hack into 500M websites :)

blocks.html_en:
The market will quickly adjust to the point where spending $50 on tokens will on average give you a vulnerability valued at $50. Maybe $100 to account for your edge in having a better prompt and the risk of prison time you take in selling the exploit

In fact that may well be true today. OP didn't try to sell it to discover the true price the market is willing to pay. And we all know that "somebody paid $$$ for something similar in the past" is no guarantee that somebody else is willing to pay any significant sum for your thing today. If it was, startups would be a lot easier

sentences.en: The market will quickly adjust to the point where spending $50 on tokens will on average give you a vulnerability valued at $50.
sentences.zh: 市场将很快调整到花费 50 美元购买代币平均会给您带来价值 50 美元的漏洞的程度。

sentences.en: Maybe $100 to account for your edge in having a better prompt and the risk of prison time you take in selling the exploitIn fact that may well be true today.
sentences.zh: 考虑到你在更好的提示方面的优势以及你因出售漏洞而入狱的风险，也许需要 100 美元。事实上，今天很可能是这样。

sentences.en: OP didn't try to sell it to discover the true price the market is willing to pay.
sentences.zh: OP 并没有试图出售它来发现市场愿意支付的真实价格。

sentences.en: And we all know that "somebody paid $$$ for something similar in the past" is no guarantee that somebody else is willing to pay any significant sum for your thing today.
sentences.zh: 我们都知道，“过去有人为类似的东西支付了 $$$”并不能保证其他人今天愿意为你的东西支付任何大笔费用。

sentences.en: If it was, startups would be a lot easier
sentences.zh: 如果是的话，创业会容易很多

blocks.html_en:
On another note - who needs WordPress in the age of Astro and LLMs?

I mean, it's not a taunt, but a serious question - do people keep WordPress because it used to be the easiest solution to set up years ago, or are there still clear use cases where one can argue it's the best solution?

sentences.en: On another note - who needs WordPress in the age of Astro and LLMs?I mean, it's not a taunt, but a serious question - do people keep WordPress because it used to be the easiest solution to set up years ago, or are there still clear use cases where one can argue it's the best solution?
sentences.zh: 另一方面 - 在 Astro 和 LLM 时代谁需要 WordPress？我的意思是，这不是一个嘲讽，而是一个严肃的问题 - 人们保留 WordPress 是因为它曾经是多年前最容易设置的解决方案，还是仍然存在明确的用例可以认为它是最好的解决方案？

content_html_zh: 没有证据表明已经或将为此类漏洞支付 50 万美元。鉴于文章称提示被修改得像圣经一样，也许可以以 50 万美元的价格出售提示。作者为 https://www.assetnote.io/ 工作，该公司拥有用于自动扫描的 AI 产品。我在该领域工作，我简直不敢相信有人会为 Word Press 漏洞支付那么多钱。 人们花钱购买 iOS 或 Android UPDATE）必须大写。>您必须指定接受长度参数的所有字段的长度。 例如，int(11)。有时，当您为 API 编写文档时，您会意识到某些内容设计得非常糟糕。 那应该发生在这里。WordPress 代码库是一个耻辱。 PHP 现在是一门美丽的语言，但他们绝对屠杀它并且拒绝对此采取任何行动。这只是因为他们不想破坏现有网站中的任何内容，就像微软通常不想破坏 Windows 上的程序一样。 因此，更改是相当渐进的，并且质量与您对一个已有数十年历史的软件的期望有关，并且没有计划如果走到这一步会发生什么。但是在这种情况下您会做什么？ 如果他们对结构的改变太多，那么要么他们无法升级现有网站，要么可能破坏网站所依赖的一大堆东西（主要是主题和插件）。 这种易于升级的方式可能会阻止很多人转向其他解决方案。即使只是为旧部件引入仿真层并选择加入新部件也比放弃并继续下去要好。 插件作者可以在发布更新或新插件和主题时选择新的子系统，作为回报，他们可以获得速度和安全保证。由于 WordPress 基金会控制着扩展市场，他们可以可靠地确定 API 表面的哪些部分正在使用，甚至每月投入一大笔资金向插件维护者发送 AI 编写的补丁以简化过渡。有很多方法可以改善这种情况（注意，这对 WordPress 维护者、客户和生态系统供应商都有好处！），但可惜的是，他们陷入了困境按照他们的方式，也不会。> 由于 WordPress 基金会控制着扩展市场，他们可以可靠地确定 API 表面的哪些部分正在使用，甚至每月投入一大笔资金向插件维护人员发送 AI 编写的补丁以简化过渡。什么是 WordPress 基金会？他们不仅破坏了经典的 WordPress 内容，而且还以令人沮丧的速度不断破坏古腾堡 API。很明显，核心 WordPress 开发人员对项目管理的想法与古腾堡开发人员截然不同。很公平。 他们添加古腾堡的方式完全是一团糟。 是的，这个想法是个好主意，如果使用得当，编辑器会非常好。 但它的建造方式肯定不理想，而且可能是世界上最糟糕的。 感觉就像有人看到 SquareSpace 和 co，认为 WordPress 落后了，并决定推动尽可能快速且低效地添加这些功能。如果现有的有风险的功能是一个安全问题，我觉得现在是一个很好的时机，可以在游戏中抛出众所周知的旗帜并使其成为一个问题。 让消费者成为恶意软件和网络钓鱼门户的宿主是最糟糕的举动。是吗？ 很久以前我用 WP 做了一个项目，当时有点困惑。 拥有一个优秀的富文本编辑器真是太好了，我得到了那部分。 但是，当人们可以直接使用 MySQL 和 PHP 时，他们为了扩展 WP 而开发的绝对怪物让我震惊了。 就像我不知道 WP 提供什么？ 它就像一个博客，如果你愿意的话，你可以痛苦地侵入其他东西。 但即使在那个时候，已经有很多很棒的电池，包括 Symfony 或 Cake 等 PHP 框架。 为什么不直接跳过痛苦的部分并直接编写你想要的代码呢？无论如何，我可能不应该发表评论，因为也许 WP 已经取得了进展，但听起来并不像这样。老实说，原因主要可以概括为“我们已经在博客中使用了 WordPress，我们现在需要在现有网站上添加一个商店，不妨扩展前者，而不是重建或维护两个系统”。或者，那些不是特别技术性的人可以拼凑出一个“足够好”满足他们需要的解决方案，而只有兴趣/能力使用一个平台的机构决定每个客户都需要使用该平台。如果你是一名熟练的程序员，WordPress 可能不是一个对于您的问题来说，这是一个很好的解决方案，但是如果您更多的是设计师或爱好者，想要一个有点老套的可扩展博客系统，那么它非常容易使用。我怀疑您错过了这个笑话：Web shell 是一个执行任意命令的脚本，即远程代码执行。哦哈哈。 是的，我的大脑以某种方式将 shell 解释为框架。但是，是的，我同意，当您需要运行快速“sudo rm -rf /”命令但无法访问终端时，WP 非常有用。我确信这是设计使然，这样围绕 WordPress 建立小企业的社区仍然留在生态系统中。 客户需要新功能？ 这真是一周的工作，因为上帝会帮助任何想要审视自己的人。据我所知，WordPress 正在积极降低网络的安全性和质量。 已经很多很多年了。他们可以更诚实一点，转向“代码就是面食”。 WP 也比米其林星级美食体验更接近披萨片。我用过的 Automattic 的所有东西都有这种感觉。 如果您想折磨工程师，只需让他们更改 WooCommerce 结帐的布局即可。作为一名大三学生，我很高兴我碰巧开始使用 PHP 版本 7。 我看了一些我们遗留下来的 PHP5 东西（谢天谢地现在都被杀掉了），它看起来非常不同。 我确信与它一起工作会很糟糕。php7 对于 PHP 来说是一个伟大的时期，老实说，那个时期也有很多伟大的实验项目（之前的 HHVM 尽管我们很少有人愿意承认，但它是迄今为止使用最多的语言。- 在网络搜索过程中仍然会出现旧教程，因此今天仍然会编写“SELECT * FROM `table` WHERE id = $id”。我认为 PHP 出现的时候正是每个人和他的狗都是“Web 开发人员”并且编写绝对无知的东西的时候。 PHP 允许这样做。ASP 有一点进入障碍，因为它需要所有的 MS。 而 PHP 却无处不在。过去也是如此。 说真的，谁制作了一个 postmeta 表并说“是的，让我们把所有东西都扔在这里。 索引？ 嗯。”我每次都感到畏缩。我不认为 PHP 是一种美丽的语言。 如果是 Laravel，就不需要重写标准库中的每个函数。 而且，与 Typescript 相比，我认为没有理由使用它。我从来没有真正理解这些对标准库的抱怨，这并不是真正的语言。 是的，它很丑陋，是的，它承载了 30 年的包袱，但 PHP
